title: Transport Updates: Lift Out of Service at Central Park Tram Stop
excerpt: Passengers using the Central Park tram stop towards Rochdale should be aware that the lift is currently out of service, affecting step-free access.
content_html:
The Central Park tram stop is experiencing a disruption as the lift on the platform towards Rochdale via Oldham is out of service. This situation has been reported since the morning of 8 July and will remain until further notice.

For those travelling towards Oldham, alternative step-free access is available via a lift from The Gateway to the Manchester bound platform. Passengers will need to use the track crossing to reach the Oldham bound platform.

In addition to this, the lift at Freehold tram stop towards Manchester is also currently out of service. Passengers are advised to use the lift from Robinson Street to access the Oldham bound platform and then cross the tracks to reach the Manchester bound platform.

These service changes are part of ongoing improvements and maintenance across the network. Passengers are encouraged to plan their journeys accordingly and check for any further updates on the TfGM travel alerts page.
legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.

excerpt: Plans for a new fire station in Heywood are advancing, aiming to enhance local emergency services. The project is part of ongoing improvements in the area.
content_html:

The initiative is part of a broader strategy to improve fire safety and response times for residents in the area.

Details regarding the exact location and timeline for the construction have yet to be disclosed, but the project is seen as a significant step for community safety.

This development reflects ongoing efforts to ensure that emergency services are well-equipped to meet the needs of the local population.

title: Plans for Temporary Fire Station in Heywood Advance
excerpt: Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service is moving forward with plans for a temporary fire station in Heywood while the existing facility is rebuilt.
content_html:
Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service (GMFRS) is set to construct a temporary fire station in Heywood as part of a redevelopment project for the existing site.

The new station will be located behind the current facility on Middleton Road, which is approximately 60 years old and deemed in need of replacement.

The temporary structure will be a two-storey modular building designed to accommodate one fire engine, while an existing outbuilding will house additional specialist vehicles.

This initiative is part of a broader £38 million Estates Strategy aimed at modernising fire stations across Greater Manchester to enhance service delivery.

Planning documents indicate that the temporary station will maintain emergency response capabilities during the redevelopment, ensuring uninterrupted service for the community.

A separate planning application for the demolition of the old station and the construction of a new community fire station, which will feature improved facilities, is expected to be submitted soon.

title: Heywood to Get Temporary Fire Station During New Facility Construction
excerpt: A temporary fire station is set to be established in Heywood while a new state-of-the-art facility is built to replace the aging current station.
content_html:
Plans are underway for a temporary fire station in Heywood as the existing facility, which has served the community for around 60 years, is deemed in need of replacement.

The fire service has confirmed that the current station no longer meets modern operational standards, prompting the decision to construct a new state-of-the-art facility.

While the new station is being built, the temporary site will ensure that fire services remain uninterrupted and accessible to the local community.

Details regarding the exact location of the temporary station and the timeline for the new facility's construction are yet to be announced, but the fire service is committed to keeping residents informed throughout the process.

title: Health Experts Advise on Fan Safety During Ongoing Heatwave
excerpt: As the UK experiences a heatwave, health professionals are offering guidance on the safe use of fans to help residents cope with rising temperatures.
content_html:
With the current heatwave affecting the UK, health experts are providing important advice regarding the use of fans to ensure safety and comfort during this period of high temperatures.

Fans can be a helpful tool for cooling down, but it is essential to use them correctly to avoid potential health risks. Experts recommend keeping fans clean and ensuring they are positioned safely to prevent accidents.

Additionally, it is advised to stay hydrated and take regular breaks from direct airflow to avoid overheating or respiratory issues. Maintaining a comfortable environment is crucial during extreme weather conditions.

Residents are encouraged to be mindful of their health and the health of others, particularly vulnerable individuals, during this heatwave. Following expert advice can help mitigate the risks associated with high temperatures.

title: Safety Tips for Using Fans During Heatwave
excerpt: As temperatures soar, health experts are advising residents on the safe use of electric fans to prevent overheating and electrical hazards.
content_html:
With the UK experiencing a significant heatwave, health and safety experts are emphasising the importance of using electric fans safely. Many households are relying on these appliances to stay cool during the hot weather.

High Speed Training, a health and safety training provider, has shared essential guidance on fan usage. Dr Richard Anderson, the organisation's Director of Learning, highlighted the need for simple precautions before operating fans.

Individuals are advised to inspect their fans for any signs of damage, particularly focusing on the plug and cable. If a fan emits unusual sounds or the plug feels warm, it should be turned off and checked by a qualified electrician.

Cleaning dust from fan blades is also recommended to reduce motor strain and prevent overheating. Fans should be unplugged during cleaning and reassembled according to the manufacturer's instructions.

Experts suggest avoiding leaving fans running unattended, especially overnight. Instead, it is better to cool bedrooms before sleeping by using fans with windows open when appropriate.

Proper positioning of fans is crucial; they should be placed on stable surfaces and kept away from curtains or other materials that could obstruct airflow.

title: Line Dance Classes Return to Rochdale Community Centre

content_html:

title: Special Edition Wembley Shirts Available for Local Supporters
excerpt: Supporters in the Rochdale area who pre-ordered a special edition Wembley shirt can collect it from the Club Shop today. For those unable to visit, shirts will be mailed out tomorrow.
content_html:
Supporters who pre-ordered a special edition Wembley shirt can pick up their items from the Club Shop today. This opportunity is specifically for those residing in the Rochdale area.

The Club Shop will be open until 5pm for collections. Fans are encouraged to visit and collect their shirts in person if possible.

For those who cannot make it to the shop before closing, there is no need to worry. The shirts will be posted out to supporters on Friday, ensuring everyone receives their order.

This initiative highlights the club's commitment to its local fanbase, providing them with a unique piece of memorabilia from a significant event.

content_html:

With nearly four decades of experience, Jaman brings a wealth of knowledge to the local martial arts scene, focusing on traditional techniques and training methods.

Training sessions will be held at Impact Gym, providing an opportunity for both beginners and experienced fighters to learn from a true expert in the field.

Muay Thai, known for its striking techniques and rigorous training, is gaining popularity in the area, and Jaman's classes aim to enhance the skills of local enthusiasts.

title: Concerns Raised Over Disability Benefits Assessment Process
excerpt: A recent report has highlighted significant issues with the current disability benefits system in the UK, describing it as inadequate and dehumanising. Experts are calling for urgent reforms to improve the experience for claimants.
content_html:
A new report has brought to light serious concerns regarding the disability benefits system in the UK. It describes the current assessment process as 'not fit for purpose' and highlights the negative impact it has on individuals seeking support.

The findings suggest that many claimants find the assessments to be dehumanising, raising questions about the effectiveness and fairness of the system. Experts are urging for immediate reforms to address these issues and improve the overall experience for those in need of assistance.

This report comes at a time when the demand for disability benefits is high, and many individuals are relying on these funds for their daily living expenses. The inadequacies of the current system could lead to further hardship for vulnerable populations.

Advocates for change are calling on policymakers to take these findings seriously and implement necessary changes to ensure that the assessment process is respectful and supportive of claimants' needs.
